note: Ladder rung 3: WA-LRU (SAGA, arXiv 2605.00528), inference-only variant. The exact three-term rule P_evict = alpha*R_hat + beta*(1-P_reuse) + gamma*S_hat, with two disclosed substitutions: P_reuse estimated from normalized access frequency (the paper uses the Agent Execution Graph; the graph-driven version is the hint-consuming rung, this is its signal-blind lower bound) and neutral default weights (the paper's tuned values could not be extracted). This row measures what the papers' best signal-blind answer collects of LRU's 60-point room over the oracle.

prediction_registered_before_run.mechanism: At the (1,1,0) default WA-LRU is a blend of recency (the alpha term, literally LRU's signal) and inverse frequency (the beta term, since our P_reuse estimator is normalized frequency). The GDSF rung already demonstrated frequency is an ANTI-signal on this workload: it over-protects the compaction-doomed early prefix, which accumulates unbeatable counts. So the beta term should HURT.
prediction_registered_before_run.point_prediction: WA-LRU-inference-only at (1,1,0) lands between LRU (160.5%) and GDSF-history (180.5%), likely worse than the LRU floor.
prediction_registered_before_run.sharper_prediction: The tuned optimum drives beta toward 0. Since beta=0 is analytically identical to LRU (P_evict = alpha*R_hat, evict-oldest), a beta=0 optimum means the inference-only reuse estimator adds NOTHING over recency alone. If the sweep shows this, it is not a null result: it is the strongest sentence yet for the hint rung's stakes. It would say the inference-only estimator of reuse probability is worthless on agentic traces, so whatever value WA-LRU's rule has must come from the workflow graph SAGA actually uses, which is exactly the information the hint interface exists to deliver. Across three signal-blind families (recency, frequency, recency+inferred-reuse), the 60 points would be shown unreachable without structural information.
prediction_registered_before_run.fresh_block_trap_check_first: A just-admitted block has age ~= 0 (protective on the alpha term) but freq = 1, so (1 - P_reuse) ~= 1 (maximally anti-protective on the beta term); at beta=1 the two fight and freshly admitted working sets can get harvested. This is inherited from the estimator substitution, not the rule. It is the mechanism to check first if the (1,1,0) number comes in ABOVE GDSF-canonical (203.2%) rather than between the floor and history.

config._ratio_note: relabeled to cap/T; denominator = measured distinct tokens T=3,698,880 (11-session, block256); earlier 0.67 used a looser ~1.835M denominator; run config unchanged
config.corpus_reproduction_check: LRU re-run on this exact cell = 160.5% (matches ladder-02), so the WA-LRU numbers below share the ladder's cell.

WALRU_default_headline.label: THE CITABLE WA-LRU-inference-only number. Neutral default, no tuning. This is the row that goes in the head-to-head table as WA-LRU's result.

WALRU_tuned.label: DISCLOSED best-case, tuned on the test set. 'WA-LRU, weights tuned on this workload - SAGA's best case under our frequency estimator' (post-hoc selection, best cell of the registered beta grid). Reported ALONGSIDE the citable 156.8, never instead of it: the head-to-head table carries both with the distinction visible, because 156.8-vs-155.4 is a 1.4-point gap on top of a ~4-point margin over the floor, and that is exactly the magnitude where tuning-disclosure decides whether a reviewer trusts the row.

0.anchor: beta=0 is analytically LRU; reproduces the floor exactly, confirming the degeneracy anchor

prediction_outcome: REFUTED, reported as such (the house genre is prediction-then-refutation-or-confirmation; this is a refutation and it stands). (1) Point prediction WRONG: WA-LRU did NOT land between LRU and GDSF-history worse than the floor; both the (1,1,0) default (156.8%) and the tuned optimum (155.4%) BEAT the LRU floor (160.5%). WA-LRU is the FIRST heuristic in the ladder to move the needle the right way. (2) Sharper prediction WRONG: the optimum is NOT beta=0. It is beta~=0.5, and the inference-only reuse estimator is worth ~5 points (160.5 -> 155.4), not zero. The claim 'inference-only reuse is worthless on agentic traces' is FALSE as stated. (3) CONFIRMED parts: the beta=0 degeneracy anchor (exactly 160.5% = LRU), and the high-beta behavior - as reuse comes to DOMINATE (beta=2: 180.4% ~= GDSF-history; beta=4: 212.3% ~= worse than GDSF-canonical), it collapses into exactly the GDSF antiquity-over-protection failure. So the GDSF mechanism was right about reuse-DOMINANT policies; it was wrong to imply reuse is useless at every weight.

revised_mechanism.the_hole_in_the_prediction: My prediction's reasoning had a hole the numbers expose. I took GDSF's evidence (frequency is an anti-signal) and generalized it to 'the beta term should hurt at ANY weight.' That was a category error: GDSF is frequency-as-PRIMARY-signal, and the U-curve shows the truth is dosage-dependent. Frequency-as-primary loses; frequency-as-subordinate-tiebreaker under a recency-dominant rule collects ~5 points. Same signal, opposite sign, depending on dose.
revised_mechanism.consistency_check: The high-beta end reproduces GDSF territory almost exactly, which is the satisfying cross-check that this is one axis and not two unrelated effects: WA-LRU at beta=2 is 180.4%, GDSF-history is 180.5% - a 0.1-point coincidence that says a reuse-dominated recency rule and a reuse-primary policy converge on the same failure. GDSF sat at the far-right of this axis all along; WA-LRU just walks the axis from LRU (beta=0) through the sweet spot (beta~=0.5) into GDSF's failure (beta>=2).
revised_mechanism.candidate_mechanism_hypothesis: Stated as hypothesis, not fact: at low beta recency governs admission and protection of the fresh working set, and the frequency term only adjudicates AMONG similarly-aged blocks - never overriding recency. Within an age cohort, 'touched more across history' plausibly separates compaction-survivors from compaction-doomed, so the correction acts exactly where recency is indifferent and is silent where recency has an opinion. That is why a subordinate dose helps where a dominant dose (which lets frequency override recency and re-protect the antique prefix) reproduces GDSF's loss.
revised_mechanism.falsifiable_edge: The hypothesis has a testable micro-claim, deliberately NOT tested now: the ~5 points should come DISPROPORTIONATELY from eviction decisions among near-tied ages (where the beta term is the tiebreaker), not from decisions where recency already separates the candidates. If a decision-level breakdown showed the gains spread evenly across age-gaps, this mechanism would be wrong. Recorded as the revision's own falsifiable edge.
revised_mechanism.status: Prediction refuted; mechanism revised to dosage-dependence; revision UNTESTED. The genre's full form is prediction -> refutation -> revised model with its own falsifiable edge, not a scoreboard correction.
revised_mechanism.fresh_block_trap_verdict: The trap was called before the run; it gets its verdict. At the default it did NOT dominate - (1,1,0) landed at 156.8%, below GDSF-canonical, so the trap is latent at recency-primary weights. But the U-curve's right side climbing steeply past beta=2 (180.4 -> 212.3) is consistent with exactly the trap mechanism activating: once beta is large enough to outvote recency, the anti-protective (1-P_reuse) term harvests freshly admitted blocks (age~=0, freq=1, so maximally evictable on the beta term). Consistent-with, not proven; the high-beta regime is where the trap would bite and the curve bends the way the trap predicts.

room_collected: Citable (default 156.8%): ~3.7 of LRU's ~60.5 points over the oracle, ~6% of the room. Disclosed best-case (tuned 155.4%): ~5.1 points, ~8.4%. Report the ~4-point citable figure as the result and the ~5-point figure as its tuned ceiling. Either way it is the first nonzero collection in the ladder and it is small: ~55 of the 60 points remain on the table for the signal-blind best-case. The scoreboard's magnitude survives even though its clean line does not.
scoreboard_after_signal_blind_tier: The signal-blind tier is complete and it is a sharper setup than either outcome of the prediction would have been. LRU 160.5%; the best signal-blind heuristic collects ~4 points citable (~5 tuned) of the 60. The rung-2 line 'zero families beat the floor' is RETIRED - WA-LRU beats it - but the thesis is intact and now has a named address: the room is real (the oracle collects all 60), the cheap access-pattern signals barely dent it (recency, frequency, recency+inferred-reuse across four families buy single-digit points and only with recency kept primary), and the remaining ~55 points require information no access-pattern statistic contains - which blocks are DEAD (lifecycle) and what blocks COST (economics). The elimination argument is now made by a prediction that was allowed to fail in public, which is worth more than the prediction holding would have been.
hint_rung_setup: The refutation strengthens the hint rung's stakes. Inference-only reuse (frequency proxy) is worth ~4-5 points; SAGA's actual signal is graph-derived P_reuse, strictly more information than frequency. WA-LRU-inference-only (156.8% citable) is now the measured floor the graph-driven version must clear to justify the hint interface. Next: the lifecycle rungs, and they start with the hint interface's degradation switches wired, because retired-cache-with-hints versus retired-cache-inference-only is the comparison the project has walked toward since the master plan named it - that pair of numbers is the thesis meeting its judge.
performance: WA-LRU ran the high-pressure cell in ~65s at beta<=1, rising to ~79s at beta=4 (more eviction churn), versus ~29s for LRU and 24.6s for GDSF on the same cell (all wall-clock include the shared oracle recompute). WA-LRU's per-eviction cost is real and now visible: every evict() recomputes max_age/max_freq/max_size and a three-term key per resident block, ~2x the LRU/GDSF replay. Still tractable; the heap/caching optimization is again declined (measured, not assumed), but for the first time the ladder has a policy whose scoring cost is worth a footnote. Receipt: beta {0,0.25,0.5,1,2,4} -> {67.1, 65.6, 65.0, 66.5, 71.1, 78.7}s real.
beta4_timeout_resolution: Recorded because silent resolution is not allowed. The first sweep ran the six cells sequentially under one 400s aggregate loop budget; at ~66s/cell that budget was exhausted after five cells and the beta=4 cell was cut off before completing (a loop-level wall limit, not a crash). It was resolved by a RERUN of beta=4 alone under a longer per-cell timeout, which completed in 78.7s -> 212.3%. Not a fixed inefficiency and not a squeak: a clean rerun with adequate time. The cell timing out at high beta is itself mild data - beta=4 is the slowest cell (78.7s vs ~65s at low beta) because reuse-domination thrashes more (hit rate drops to 90.2%, more evictions to score), so scoring cost rises with beta. A real high-beta cost, not a pathology that changes the number.
